weeks.weekly_summary: Establish aerobic base with 2 sessions per week on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday). Recent Strava data shows ~15.7 km over 3 activities in 14 days; Week 1 targets ~16-18 km to settle into the 4-hour budget. One quality session (tempo) introduces threshold work.
weeks.coaching_notes: Athlete is returning from a light training phase (recent avg 5.2 km per run). Week 1 anchors the block at a sustainable base. Monday easy run + Wednesday tempo build rhythm. Cross-training (Pilates Tue, Gym Fri, Football Sat) are blocked; rest on Thu/Sun. Target paces are tight (0:04 easy, 0:03 tempo/intervals), reflecting a strong 2:00 HM fitness level.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 increases volume ~8-10% to ~19 km. Add a second quality session (intervals on Monday or Wednesday). Long run emerges on the non-quality day.

days.title: Easy 7km
days.description: Conversational pace easy run to activate aerobic system. Target pace: 0:04/km. Focus on relaxed cadence and steady breathing.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Light core/flexibility work. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: Tempo 8km (3km @ 0:03/km)
days.description: 2km easy warm-up (0:04/km) + 3km tempo at lactate threshold pace (0:03/km) + 3km easy cool-down (0:04/km). Teaches threshold effort in a Base Building phase.
days.notes: First quality session. Keep tempo portion controlled; focus on sustainable effort.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or very light mobility work (5–10 min easy walking).
days.notes: Prepare for hard session on Wednesday.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym
days.description: Strength/conditioning session. Blocked cross-training day. Light weights or bodyweight focus.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: High-intensity intermittent sport. Blocked cross-training day.
days.notes: Counts as high-intensity stimulus; do not run today.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Recovery day after Football (Sat).

weeks.weekly_summary: Volume increases ~8% to ~19 km. Introduce a second quality session (intervals on Monday). Long run emerges on Wednesday (~9 km at long-run pace). Polarized structure solidifies: 2 hard sessions + easy runs.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 2 begins building aerobic capacity and introduces intervals. Monday = intervals (VO2 max stimulus); Wednesday = long run at sustainable long-run pace (0:03/km). Total time stays within 4-hour budget. Keep intensity controlled—this is still Base Building. Football (Sat) continues as cross-training.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 peaks the block: volume +5–8% to ~20 km. Long run extends to ~10–11 km. One quality session (tempo or intervals). Cumulative fatigue rises—monitor recovery.

days.title: Intervals: 6 × 800m @ 0:03/km
days.description: 2km easy warm-up (0:04/km) + 6 × 800m @ 0:03/km with 2 min recovery jog (0:04/km) + 1km easy cool-down (0:04/km). VO2 max development.
days.notes: Second quality session of block. Maintain consistent effort on repeats.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Light core/flexibility work. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: Long Run 9km @ 0:03/km
days.description: Sustained aerobic effort at long-run pace (0:03/km). Focus on maintaining steady effort and fluid running form. Builds aerobic base and mental toughness for half marathon.
days.notes: First long run of block. Pace is consistent and conversational.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or very light mobility work.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym
days.description: Strength/conditioning session. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: High-intensity intermittent sport. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Recovery day after Football (Sat).

weeks.weekly_summary: Peak week of the block: volume increases ~5–8% to ~20.5 km. Long run extends to 10–11 km at long-run pace. One quality session (tempo) maintains lactate-threshold fitness. Cumulative fatigue rises; monitor recovery closely.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 3 is the cumulative peak before the recovery week. Long run on Wednesday reaches 10.5 km (growth of 1.5 km from W2). Monday = tempo run (lactate threshold). Total volume remains within time budget. Fatigue management is critical: ensure sleep, hydration, and nutrition support the increased load. Football (Sat) continues as high-intensity cross-training stimulus.
recovery_flags: Week 3 is peak load week. Monitor HRV, resting HR, and perceived fatigue closely.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 is RECOVERY: volume drops 20–25% to ~15–16 km. Keep one quality session (short tempo or fartlek). Reduce long run by 20% (~8.5 km). Consolidate fitness before next training block.

days.title: Tempo 8km (4km @ 0:03/km)
days.description: 2km easy warm-up (0:04/km) + 4km tempo @ 0:03/km (lactate threshold pace) + 2km easy cool-down (0:04/km). Extended tempo block builds sustained lactate-threshold capacity.
days.notes: Quality session #3 of block. Maintain control on tempo section.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Light core/flexibility work. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: Long Run 10.5km @ 0:03/km
days.description: Steady aerobic effort at long-run pace (0:03/km). Build towards half-marathon distance (~21 km). Maintain conversational pace and fluid form throughout.
days.notes: Peak long run of block. +1.5 km from Week 2.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. 1 day after long run.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym
days.description: Strength/conditioning session. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: High-intensity intermittent sport. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Recovery day after Football (Sat). Prepare mentally for recovery week.

weeks.weekly_summary: RECOVERY WEEK: Volume drops 20–25% to ~15.5 km (vs ~20.5 km in W3). Maintain one quality session (short fartlek) to sustain fitness while allowing physical/mental recovery. Long run reduced by 20% to 8.5 km. This consolidates the block's gains before progressing to the next training phase.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 4 is a planned recovery week. Reduced volume allows the body to adapt and supercompensate. Monday = short fartlek (fun, varied-pace workout) instead of long tempo. Wednesday = short long run (8.5 km, down 19% from W3). Intensity is lower; focus is on recovery, mobility, and sleep. Cross-training continues (Pilates Tue, Gym Fri, Football Sat). After Week 4, transition to the next 4-week block (Aerobic Development phase).
recovery_flags: Recovery week: volume reduced to allow adaptation. Prioritize sleep (8+ hours), hydration, and nutrition.
weeks.next_week_preview: After Week 4, next block (Weeks 5–8, Aerobic Development phase) begins. Volume builds again to ~22–24 km/week. Long run extends towards 12+ km. Tempo and threshold work increase in frequency and duration.

days.title: Fartlek 7km (varied pace)
days.description: 2km easy warm-up (0:04/km) + 5 × (1 min @ 0:03/km + 1 min easy @ 0:04/km) + 1km easy cool-down (0:04/km). Unstructured intervals; focus on feel and recovery.
days.notes: Light quality session. Keep effort conversational.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Light core/flexibility work. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: Long Run 8.5km @ 0:03/km
days.description: Steady, conversational-pace aerobic run (0:03/km). 19% reduction from W3 peak (10.5 km). Maintain consistency but dial back intensity. Enjoy the process.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or very light mobility work (5–10 min easy walking).

days.title: 🏋️ Gym
days.description: Strength/conditioning session. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: High-intensity intermittent sport. Blocked cross-training day.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Recovery day after Football (Sat). Reflect on the block; prepare mentally for Aerobic Development phase.
days.notes: End of Base Building block. Next block begins Week 5.

coaching_overview: This 4-week Base Building block (14 weeks to Half Marathon race) establishes aerobic foundation and introduces quality sessions at target paces. Week 1 (17.5 km) anchors the athlete's 4-hour weekly budget; weeks 2–3 build progressively to a peak of 20.5 km; week 4 recovers at 15.5 km. Polarized structure: 80% easy (0:04/km), 20% quality (0:03/km tempo/intervals). Two runs per week (Monday, Wednesday preferred days) fit neatly into the schedule, with Pilates (Tue), Gym (Fri), and Football (Sat) as fixed cross-training commitments. Training paces align with a strong 2:00 HM fitness level (0:03/km race pace). After Week 4, the next block (Weeks 5–8, Aerobic Development) ramps tempo work and extends long runs towards 12+ km, progressing toward Peak Training (Weeks 9–12). Monitor fatigue weekly; adjust intensity if HRV trends down or resting HR spikes.
weekly_summary: Establish aerobic base with 2 sessions per week on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday). Recent Strava data shows ~15.7 km over 3 activities in 14 days; Week 1 targets ~16-18 km to settle into the 4-hour budget. One quality session (tempo) introduces threshold work.
coaching_notes: Athlete is returning from a light training phase (recent avg 5.2 km per run). Week 1 anchors the block at a sustainable base. Monday easy run + Wednesday tempo build rhythm. Cross-training (Pilates Tue, Gym Fri, Football Sat) are blocked; rest on Thu/Sun. Target paces are tight (0:04 easy, 0:03 tempo/intervals), reflecting a strong 2:00 HM fitness level.
